response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer (~13+ years) but has only ~6 months of C/C++ exposure from National Instruments back in 2014–2015. The role centers on C++/STL, multi-threaded networked systems, MQTT, IEC 62443 SDL, and industrial automation — none of which appear in the recent career history, which is dominated by Node.js, TypeScript, and DLang. Strong engineering fundamentals and craftsmanship, but the core stack mismatch is too large for a mid-senior C++ position.
strengths: 13+ years of professional software engineering with senior/staff-level roles
Strong testing, TDD, and software craftsmanship culture (workshops, dojos, CI/CD)
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput services (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) and distributed/event-driven systems
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience — only a short stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015
No evidence of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity exposure
No experience with MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850/104, DNP 3.0, or industrial automation domain

key_factors.factor: General senior engineering experience and craftsmanship
